vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but shows significant degradation. P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) is present. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are in the black pot, but show signs of systemic decline.

plant_audit.P1: String of Nickels: Stable, low density, minimal growth observed.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Mexican Mint/Silver Guest: High stress. Significant leaf loss and chlorosis observed over the 5-day period.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos: One large leaf remains, showing signs of senescence (yellowing/browning). The rabbit anchor is stable.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently damp with some surface crusting in the P3/P4 pot.
biome_observations.debris: White perlite-like particles are scattered across the surface of all pots. No fungal blooms detected.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No new sprouts or weeds identified.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Initial observation shows a large, yellowing leaf on P3. P2/P4 shows a single, upright green leaf.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_CURRENT: The P3 leaf has become more necrotic (brown/yellow). The P2/P4 upright leaf remains, but the surrounding soil shows no new vegetative activity.

vision_report.anomalies: The P3 leaf is showing advanced senescence. The lack of new growth in the P2/P4 pot suggests a potential root-zone issue or dormancy.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a chronological audit of the biome. First, I cataloged the specimens against the registry, then I performed a pixel-by-pixel comparison of the leaf health and soil surface. Validation: The P3 leaf has progressively lost chlorophyll, confirming a decline in health. The P2/P4 pot remains stagnant. The biome is currently in a state of low-metabolic activity or decline.
